summary: Second straight steady-state week. Portfolio health holds at 88 with zero broken projects and no open BLOCK, confirming the recovery from the 06-08/06-15/06-22 commit-stall era is durable, not a one-week bounce. Engineering moved on both games: BR shipped three substantive releases (v0.59.7-.9: fog-hitch fix, thirteen hot-path optimizations, A-star and spatial-index extraction) and HBH closed its O(n) targeting-scan scale findings via a v0.99.17 spatial hash (performance 74 to 82, scale-architecture 62 to 75). The cross-cutting story is the dashboard-writer tooling: the same script family flagged for timing-staleness last week produced a concrete display HIGH (BR's audit block null since 06-11 from a write-dashboard-status.ps1 glob bug), with a sibling in YaS (route-count regex reports 0). Two process items carry forward unresolved: the concurrent per-project-vs-constellation collision (06-28 action 2) and un-pushed local-ahead commits, which spread from BR to YaS, YaA, and YaB. The 20 open actions are granular hygiene (HBH 12, BR 5, YaS 3), not breakage.

topActions.label: Fix the shared dashboard-writer glob/regex family: BR's audit block reads null since 06-11 (write-dashboard-status.ps1:53 globs AUDIT-*.md, never matches CANONICAL_AUDIT-*.md) and YaS's route-count regex reports api routes 0 against 20 real routes; same script lineage, same bug class
Push the un-pushed local-ahead commits on YaS (2), YaA (2), and YaB (2) so HEAD and origin reconcile; all dashboard-writer plumbing

Refresh HBH project metadata to the post-v0.98.5 native-3D stack: rewrite the hazard catalog and fix .project-context.json critical-files and scheduled-task entries that still describe the retired 2D build
Fix the dead recovery-script pointers in publish-gdd.ps1 and CLAUDE.md so the incident runbook works at incident time
Sweep the GDD changelog body of per-Nick / the-user framing and AI tool names, and extend the voice audit to scan the full body
Serialize or stagger the scheduled per-project bar-raise tasks against the weekly constellation; the concurrent-unattended-session hazard recurred this run (carried from 06-28 action 2, unresolved)
Capture one peak-wave frame profile on a v0.59.9 build to set the M4 60-FPS baseline; three perf commits and an algorithmic change shipped with no profile
Make the canonical audit release-aware: post-commit audit pass at the end of release.ps1 so an evening ship is re-checked same day
